title: Dynamic Glazing Tint Levels
summary: Dynamic architectural glazing typically corresponds to four visible light transmittance levels: 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60%.
evidence_quote: Typically, the tint corresponds to 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60% visible light transmittance Tvis depending on manufacturers.

title: Dynamic Glazing Application Feasibility
summary: Dynamic glazing is most feasible on large glazing facades not facing north, and works well on east and west orientations.
evidence_quote: Dynamic glazing is usually the most feasible on large glazing facades not do not face north. It works well on east and west orientations since these are difficult to horizontally shade.

title: Dynamic Glazing South Facade Use
summary: Dynamic glazing can also be used efficiently on south facades that are not shaded.
evidence_quote: It could also be used efficiently on south facades that are not shaded.

title: Desiccant Dehumidification Dew Point Threshold
summary: Applications requiring a dew point at or below 40°F (4.3°C) may be candidates for active desiccant dehumidification.
evidence_quote: In general, applications that require a dew point at or below 40°F (4.3°C) may be candidates for active desiccant dehumidification.

title: Desiccant Dehumidification Application Examples
summary: Examples of candidates for desiccant dehumidification include facilities handling hygroscopic materials, film drying, and manufacturing of candy, chocolate, drugs, chemicals, plastics, electronics, and packaging of moisture-sensitive products.
evidence_quote: Examples of such candidates include facilities handling hygroscopic materials; film drying; the manufacturing of candy, chocolate, or chewing gum; the manufacturing of drugs and chemicals; the manufacturing of plastic materials; packaging of moisture-sensitive products; and the manufacturing of electronics.

title: Desiccant Dehumidification in Supermarkets
summary: Supermarkets often use desiccant dehumidification to avoid condensation on refrigerated casework.
evidence_quote: Supermarkets often use desiccant dehumidification to avoid condensation on refrigerated casework.

title: Rotary Desiccant Dehumidifier Operation
summary: Rotary desiccant dehumidifiers use solid desiccants like silica gel; humid process air is dehumidified in one part of the bed while a different part is dried for reuse by reactivation air.
evidence_quote: Rotary desiccant dehumidifiers use solid desiccants such as silica gel to attract water vapor from the moist air. Humid air, generally referred to as process air, is dehumidified in one part of the desiccant bed while a different part of the bed is dried for reuse by a second airstream known as reactivation air.

title: Desiccant Process Air Outlet Temperature
summary: The process air outlet temperature is higher than the inlet temperature primarily because the heat of sorption of the moisture removed is converted to sensible heat.
evidence_quote: The process air outlet temperature is higher than the inlet temperature primarily because the heat of sorption of the moisture removed is converted to sensible heat.

title: Solar Thermal Application Temperature Ranges
summary: Solar thermal applications range from low-temperature (swimming pool heating or domestic hot water) up to medium- or high-temperature applications (space heating, absorption cooling or steam production for electrical generation).
evidence_quote: Solar energy thermal applications range from low-temperature applications (swimming pool heating or domestic hot water) up to medium- or high-temperature applications (space heating, absorption cooling or steam production for electrical generation).

title: Solar Combi System Components
summary: A typical installation for the combined production of DHW and space heating (solar combi systems) includes solar collectors, a heat storage tank, and a boiler used as an auxiliary heater.
evidence_quote: A typical installation for the combined production of DHW and space heating (i.e., solar combi systems) includes the solar collectors, the heat storage tank, and a boiler used as an auxiliary heater.
